title: Ononis pedicellaris
intro: Ononis pedicellaris, commonly known as the glandular spurry, is a perennial herbaceous plant belonging to the family Fabaceae. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, helping to alleviate symptoms of various conditions such as arthritis and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Ononis pedicellaris include alkaloids, flavonoids, and glycosides, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used in herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, often combined with other herbs to enhance its therapeutic effects.
taxonomy: Ononis pedicellaris belongs to the family Fabaceae, commonly known as legumes or pea family. It is a genus of flowering plants in the tribe Genisteae. Within the Fabaceae family, Ononis pedicellaris is a part of the subfamily Faboideae. The subfamily Faboideae is further divided into various tribes, with Genisteae being one of them. Ononis pedicellaris's classification reflects its evolutionary relationships with other plants within the Fabaceae family.
uses: Ononis pedicellaris is not commonly referenced in most standard sources on medicinal plants, however, some information on Ononis species, which are part of the legume family, suggests that certain species within this genus have been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments.  Some Ononis species have been reported to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
